articles.title: We Spent Months Cleaning SEC EDGAR 13F Data So You Don't Have To
articles.excerpt: The data isn't the hard part. Cleaning it is. SEC EDGAR is public, free, and a mess. Every quarter, 13,000+ institutional investment managers file Form 13F, disclosing their U.S. equity holdings. In t…
articles.body: The data isn't the hard part. Cleaning it is. SEC EDGAR is public, free, and a mess. Every quarter, 13,000+ institutional investment managers file Form 13F, disclosing their U.S. equity holdings. In theory that's a beautiful dataset — every hedge fund, every pension fund, every bank, all in one place. In practice, the raw filings actively fight you: The same institution files under different names, sometimes even in the same quarter. In our own database right now: BlackRock, Inc. and BlackRock Inc. are two distinct CIK registrations for what most people would call "one" institution. Multiply that across 13,000+ filers and you get a long tail of near-duplicate names that break any naive GROUP BY institution_name . CUSIPs don't map cleanly to tickers.
articles.author: James Hsiao

articles.title: How I Built an SSR Valorant Tracker with React, Supabase and Live Esports Data
articles.excerpt: How I Built an SSR Valorant Tracker with React, Supabase and Live Esports Data I recently built VALTRAIN, a player-focused Valorant platform that combines a Valorant Tracker, VCT match database and we…
articles.body: How I Built an SSR Valorant Tracker with React, Supabase and Live Esports Data I recently built VALTRAIN , a player-focused Valorant platform that combines a Valorant Tracker, VCT match database and weapon skins explorer. The project started as a simple match history lookup page. It eventually became a much larger SSR application with player statistics, esports schedules, match detail pages, replay discovery, multilingual routes and searchable cosmetic data. The Main Product Areas VALTRAIN is divided into three primary areas. 1. Valorant Tracker The Valorant Tracker accepts a Riot ID and region.
articles.author: 거북이

articles.title: Fool us three times? Galaxy Watch owners are reporting abnormal battery drain yet again
articles.excerpt: It's been a horrible year for battery life if you're a Galaxy Watch owner.
articles.body: Credit: Tushar Mehta / Android Authority TL;DR Some Galaxy Watch owners are reporting major battery drain issues. The problem seems to affect multiple models, including the Galaxy Watch 6, 7, and 8 lines. This is at least the third time this year that a flood of Galaxy Watch owners have reported abnormal battery drain. Samsung has just launched the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 , but it’s been a topsy-turvy year if you own an older Galaxy Watch. These users reported battery drain issues in 2026, and it sounds like the problem has struck again. Multiple Reddit users have reported major battery drain problems on their Samsung smartwatches. The problem seems to affect a variety of devices, including the Galaxy Watch 8, Galaxy Watch 7, and Galaxy Watch 6 generations.
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: The Hidden Part of Refresh Token Implementation that every developers should know
articles.excerpt: What happens when 5 parallel API calls hit for an expired JWT at the exact same millisecond. Imagine this: You’ve built a sleek, high-performance React dashboard. The UI is sharp, dark mode is gleamin…
articles.body: What happens when 5 parallel API calls hit for an expired JWT at the exact same millisecond. Imagine this: You’ve built a sleek, high-performance React dashboard. The UI is sharp, dark mode is gleaming, components are modularized, and React Query is executing parallel data fetches like a grand symphony. You brew a cup of coffee, open the app after lunch, hit refresh, and… BAM! You are immediately booted back to the Login screen. No warnings, no friendly error toasts—just a cold, ruthless redirect. You check your JWT expiration timer. The access token died 5 seconds ago, but your refresh token is valid for another 14 days. So why on earth did your app decide to kick you out like an uninvited party crasher?
articles.author: Nilesh Kumar

articles.title: The prompt didn't replace drag & drop
articles.excerpt: In GoodBarber, you build an app without writing code: you assemble components, drag and drop, with a design system holding everything coherent underneath. Recently, a second way in appeared: you can g…
articles.body: In GoodBarber, you build an app without writing code: you assemble components, drag and drop, with a design system holding everything coherent underneath. Recently, a second way in appeared: you can get a custom section by describing it to an AI in plain language. Two ways of creating now live in the same back office — and the question comes immediately: is this the beginning of the end for drag & drop? No. And that "no" isn't a defensive reflex — it's an interface-architecture choice you can reason about. The ladder everyone knows The history of creation tools gets told the same way by everyone, and it's true: a ladder of abstraction. First, code — total control, total complexity.
articles.author: Dominique Siacci

articles.title: Back-of-the-envelope estimation for system design interviews
articles.excerpt: Back-of-the-envelope estimation for system design interviews Most people don't fail capacity math because the arithmetic is hard. They fail because they do it silently, produce a number they can't def…
articles.body: Back-of-the-envelope estimation for system design interviews Most people don't fail capacity math because the arithmetic is hard. They fail because they do it silently, produce a number they can't defend, and then never use it again for the rest of the interview. The math itself is trivial. The method is what's worth learning. Why interviewers ask Capacity estimation isn't a numeracy test. It's checking two things: Can you tell whether a design is physically possible before you commit to it? Do you know which constraint actually binds — storage, read throughput, write throughput, or bandwidth? A candidate who estimates 30,000 reads/sec and 200 writes/sec has learned something that changes the design.
articles.author: TierOnePrep

articles.body: Live Project: tv.cadnative.com What Is FireViston TV? FireViston TV is a full-stack streaming solution designed for the modern living room. It consists of two parts that work in tight coordination: FireViston TV Android App — a native Android/Android TV application that delivers a polished, remote-friendly viewing experience. https://github.com/akshaynikhare/FireVisionIPTV/releases FireViston TV Server — the backend streaming infrastructure that powers content delivery, user management, and playback control hosted at tv.cadnative.com Together, they form a complete, self-contained streaming platform. The Android App Built for the 10-Foot Experience The FireViston Android app is designed for television screens — large fonts, d-pad navigation, and a layout that works from across the room.

articles.body: TL;DR Google has started rolling out Fitbit Air firmware version 253.2. The update only includes minor bug fixes, with no new features or user-facing changes mentioned in Google’s announcement. Google recommends installing the update during the day and keeping the Google Health app open throughout the process. Google has started rolling out a new software update for the Fitbit Air . However, this release is more about improving reliability than introducing new features. According to Google’s community announcement , firmware version 253.2 is now making its way to Fitbit Air devices over the next few days. The company says the update contains minor bug fixes, without going into specifics of what those fixes include.

articles.excerpt: Google's powerful digital assistant is now reaching more users across the globe.
articles.body: TL;DR After its limited initial rollout, Google is expanding access to Gemini Spark starting today. Google AI Pro subscribers in the US can now use Spark, while AI Ultra subscribers are getting access globally with local language support. The rollout still excludes users in the EEA, UK, Switzerland, and Nigeria. Google is significantly expanding access to Gemini Spark , its AI-powered task delegation feature, after introducing it to a limited group of users earlier this year.

articles.title: 'It's Being Written, Is the Short Answer' — Zach Cregger Gives an Update on the Aunt Gladys Prequel | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Director Zach Cregger said that the Aunt Gladys-focused Weapons prequel movie is in the works and will have "lots of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things" at San Diego Comic-Con.
articles.body: Weapons and upcoming Resident Evil director Zach Cregger gave an update on the status of his Aunt Gladys prequel film at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday afternoon. "First of all, I'm really excited about the Gladys movie. I loved working with Amy [Madigan], I love that character," Cregger said. "For the movie Weapons, it really made a lot of sense not to dive into her backstory because the device of Weapons was to keep her very opaque and very mysterious. However, I think there's an amazing story to be told about where Gladys comes from and how she became what we know her as. It's got a lot of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things, kind of like Weapons does, and I think it's gonna be great.
articles.author: Leanne Butkovic

The image in question was what Montgomery called the "Avengers shot," with the characters posing together that leaked out of CinemaCon in 2024 before it was taken down. "This was one of the very first funny stories about this image. We had a version of this image, exact same painting. The characters looked a little bit different," Montgomery said. articles.excerpt: The filmmaker discusses Alan Ritchson, visual storytelling, unforgettable needle drops, and why "the first person to speak usually loses."
articles.body: After spending nearly two decades in development, Motor City has finally reached the big screen. Starring Alan Ritchson as an ex-Army Ranger seeking revenge after being framed and sent to prison, the film tells much of its story with little dialogue, relying instead on physical performances, visual storytelling, and a soundtrack of classic rock needle drops that become as important as the characters themselves.

articles.title: League of Legends Riftbound TCG: First Impressions of the Vendetta Booster Set | IGN Preview
articles.excerpt: Get an early look at League of Legends Riftbound's upcoming Vendetta expansion.
articles.body: The wait is finally over for Riftbound’s Vendetta booster set, as its release is now just around the corner. This will be the League of Legends TCG’s fourth big drop with pre-release events at local stores starting July 24th with the global release on July 31st. That’s right! With the game having about a year under its belt now, fans finally have a synced-up release with both English and Chinese languages for the first time. Not only is this great for the meta, but players will also have some new deck combinations to mess around with as the set introduces new legends with opposing colors or domains and, of course, new mechanics like Flow to give some extra options out of hand.

articles.body: Matthew Needham is the voice of The Joker in Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2, Prime Video has announced. British actor Needham is most famous for playing Larys Strong in Game of Thrones prequel series House of the Dragon. Confirmation comes from the Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 panel at San Diego Comic-Con, which ended with a trailer for Amazon’s upcoming Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles game . It’s exclusive to Amazon Luna (and now, part of Amazon Prime ), and launches alongside all 10 episodes of Season 2 on July 31. Batman: Caped Crusader is a superhero noir animated series created by Bruce Timm (co-creator of Batman: The Animated Series), The Batman director Matt Reeves, and J.J. Abrams.
articles.author: Wesley Yin-Poole

articles.title: Amazon Prime Has an Exclusive Batman Video Game Set to Launch Alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Amazon and Warner Bros. have announced Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles, a new Batman game exclusive to Amazon Prime set to launch on July 31 alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2.
articles.body: Amazon and Warner Bros. have announced Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles, a new Batman game exclusive to Amazon Prime set to launch on July 31 alongside Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 . Both the game and the new season of the animated show will be available for Amazon Prime members at no additional cost. The game is part of Amazon Luna’s move into Prime Video, which launched today ahead of the Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 panel at San Diego Comic-Con. Batman: Caped Crusader - Chronicles is a cooperative crime-solving party game for up to four players. The idea is that people will watch Batman: Caped Crusader Season 2 then play the game, using their phones as controllers.
articles.author: Wesley Yin-Poole

articles.title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
articles.excerpt: NASA's SunRISE mission has been assigned a new launch vehicle: It will now be delivered to orbit by a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket rather than a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur.
articles.body: NASA is shaking things up for its next mission to research the sun. The six satellites of NASA's Sun Radio Interferometer Space Experiment (SunRISE) mission are poised to launch to Earth orbit soon, to serve as an early warning system for incoming solar storms. They were originally slated to lift off on a United Launch Alliance (ULA) Vulcan Centaur rocket but have now been assigned a SpaceX Falcon Heavy , according to a NASA mission update . SunRISE will fly to space as part of a rideshare mission sponsored by the U.S. Space Force's (USSF's) Space Systems Command (SSC). Though it's not stated specifically in the NASA release, that may be a hint as to why the mission's rocket was changed.
articles.author: Josh Dinner

articles.title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
articles.excerpt: 'We've seen a lot of strange new worlds, but we’ll never see something like this again'
articles.body: 2026 is the 60th anniversary of "Star Trek". To help celebrate the show that creator Gene Roddenberry described as "Wagon Train to the Stars,""Star Trek: Strange New Worlds" returns to Paramount+ for its penultimate season. From teasers and trailers that first dropped last summer and fall, we’re anticipating a rollicking batch of dynamic episodes that draw on diverse subgenres, with everything from classic film noir crime stories and time travel to spooky ghost stories and sword-clashing swashbucklers. But the end is nigh for this colorful modern interpretation of "Trek". Next year's truncated fifth season will be the crew's final mission, so let's revel in the time we have left and beam onto the Enterprise bridge for a promising round of missions.
articles.author: Jeff Spry

articles.title: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of the sun's missing silver
articles.excerpt: Scientists say the sun has a little more silver than we thought.
articles.body: For several years now, astronomers who study the sun have faced a little mystery — our star seemed to hold too little silver. Whenever scientists examined the sun's outer layers, they've seen significantly less silver than they've expected to find. Where, then, did the missing silver go? As it turns out, this mystery may finally be solved. Newly published research suggests that the sun 's missing silver may have been hiding in plain sight all along. At first, the real mystery might seem to be why you'd seek silver in the sun at all. After all, 98.5% of the sun's mass is made from lightweight hydrogen and helium. Silver is just a tiny fraction of the remaining 1.5%, which also includes traces of other heavy elements like iron and copper.
articles.author: Rahul Rao

articles.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
articles.excerpt: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organizat…
articles.body: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor recovery. Opening the message was enough to start it. The NSA, CISA and partner agencies published
